name: Banana Cake with Penuche Frosting
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the cake flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt into a bowl.
Blend well and set aside.
2. In a measuring cup, mix vinegar with enough milk to make 2/3 cup.
Set aside.
3. Cream the shortening and sugar in a large mixing bowl using an electric mixer on medium speed until light and fluffy.
4. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
5. Alternately add the dry ingredients mixture and milk mixture with bananas to the creaming mixture, blending well after each addition.
6. Stir in chopped walnuts and pour batter into a greased 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan.
7. Bake preheated oven at 350°F for about 45 minutes or until a cake tester inserted in the center comes out clean.
8. Cool on a wire rack until cold to the touch, then frost.
Penuche Frosting:
1. Heat butter in a 2-quart saucepan over medium heat until bubbling but not browned.
2. Stir in brown sugar and cook stirring constantly until mixture boils.
3. Reduce heat to low and cook another 2 minutes while stirring constantly.
4. Stir in milk, then increase the heat to medium and continue cooking until mixture returns to a boil.
5. Remove from the heat and cool to lukewarm.
6. When mixture is lukewarm, stir in confectioners' sugar and beat with a wooden spoon until well blended and thick enough to spread.
7. If frosting becomes too stiff, place pan in a bowl of ice water and continue beating until frosting reaches spreading consistency. If necessary, warm over low heat.
